narration: T cells hunt infected cells and coordinate immune responses. But how do they do it?
visual_description: Hook visual: T cells hunt infected cells and coordinate immune responses. But how do they do it?

narration: T cells are a type of white blood cell crucial for your immune defense. They recognize and destroy cells that are infected.

narration: Imagine tiny warriors navigating your body, seeking out threats. This is how T cells patrol your system.

narration: Recent studies show T cells also help other immune cells function better, like a conductor in an orchestra.
